# invisible-string

> An open-source, self-hostable platform for AI agents: hire an agent — a persona, a model, and the tools it's equipped with — chat with it like a teammate, or delegate standing work with workflows fired from Slack, forms, webhooks, or a schedule. Every published agent compiles into a self-hosted, version-pinned process; runs stream live, pause for human approval, and survive infrastructure failure without losing work.

## Docs

- [Overview](/docs/getting-started/overview): what invisible-string is and how the pieces fit
- [Quickstart](/docs/getting-started/quickstart): hire, publish, chat, delegate
- [Agents](/docs/concepts/agents): persona, model, context — and how publishing versions them
- [Workflows](/docs/concepts/workflows): standing delegations — trigger, agent, instructions
- [Triggers](/docs/building/triggers): chat, webhook, form, Slack, and schedule ingress
- [Architecture](/docs/platform/architecture): control plane, stateless workers, compiler
- [Durability](/docs/platform/durability): runs survive worker death and resume anywhere

## Source

- [GitHub](https://github.com/heysanil/invisible-string): repository and issue tracker
